title: Statements and Operating Policies - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This is a listing/index page from the New York Fed that aggregates dozens of separate statements about open market operations, repurchase agreements, SOFR methodology, and related market plumbing topics spanning 2022-2026. The page itself is not a single archiveable document but rather an index to individual statements that should be processed separately.
key_points: This is a navigation page containing links to approximately 80+ separate operational statements, not a single source document.
Statements cover diverse market structure topics including repo operations, SOFR methodology, FedTrade platform changes, and securities lending.
Recent items (2026) include FedTrade Plus usage for SOMA and Treasury buybacks, and reference rate publication procedures.
Earlier items include Standing Repo Facility updates, SOMA reduction plans, and IOSCO compliance statements.
No single publication date, URL, or author applies to the page as a whole.
why_it_matters: This listing page is useful for discovering individual New York Fed operational statements but cannot serve as a standalone research source. Each linked statement addresses specific market structure mechanics such as overnight repo operations, SOFR calculation changes, or FedTrade platform updates that would each warrant separate archival treatment.
market_structure_context: The page aggregates statements on market plumbing topics including: repurchase agreement operations (standing overnight repo, small value exercises), SOFR methodology modifications, FedTrade platform changes, securities lending facilities, SOMA holdings management, and counterparty eligibility criteria for the Standing Repo Facility.

issues_found: Source is a listing/index page containing ~80+ separate operational statements - not a single archiveable document
